Reason_for_Visit.summary: Metastatic breast cancer, stage not specified, hormone therapy follow-up

Clinical_Findings.findings: Stable to slightly decreased metabolic activity of osseous metastases. No new metastases are identified. No palpable masses, skin changes, or lymphadenopathy noted on physical exam. Imaging findings: multiple osseous lesions involving the thoracolumbar and sternum are not changed in size since prior study and do not demonstrate hypermetabolic activity, consistent with treated metastases. Left iliac wing sclerotic lesion is unchanged in size and metabolic activity with an SUV of 3.1. Persistent FDG uptake within left L1 pedicle (SUV 6) and anterior vertebral body (SUV 9) is not significantly changed since prior.

Treatment_Changes.recent_changes: Hormone positive MBC to bone with stable disease.

Treatment_Goals.current_meds: Femara, Zoladex, calcium-vitamin D, zolendronic acid

Response_Assessment.response_assessment: stable disease, with stable to slightly decreased metabolic activity of osseous metastases on PET-CT
Response_Assessment.current_meds: Femara, Zoladex, calcium-vitamin D, zolendronic acid

Medication_Plan.current_meds: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.

Procedure_Plan.next_steps: Reassess lower back pain at two weeks, Obtain CBC with platelets, Obtain UA

Imaging_Plan.imaging_plan: MRI of the spine in 2 weeks

Genetic_Testing_Plan.genetic_testing_plan: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.

follow_up_next_visit.Purpose of next clinic visit: to reassess lower back pain and consider MRI of the spine
follow_up_next_visit.Current symptoms: lower back pain, easy bruising, possible UTI, genital-urinary symptoms
